name: Summary Renderer

tasks.title: Implement summary renderer for replay mode
tasks.description: Implement summary renderer for replay mode
tasks.body: Create internal/logrender/summary.go. Implement a SummaryRenderer struct that accepts an io.Writer for output. The Replay(records <-chan Record) method consumes records and produces the summary format: one line per completed stage. The renderer must track stage_start records to pair them with stage_complete records for duration calculation. Output format per the spec: '[stage-label] node-address (duration)' with column alignment. The stage label comes from StageLabel(). Node address includes the task path when present (node/task-id). Use ✓ for exit_code 0 and ✗ for non-zero. For audit_report_written records, print the report path indented on the next line: '  report: <path>'. planning_start/planning_complete should be rendered as [plan] stages. Skip terminal_marker, assistant, and other record types. Filter out daemon_start/daemon_stop records (they are not stages).

tasks.title: Add follow mode to summary renderer
tasks.description: Add follow mode to summary renderer
tasks.body: Extend the SummaryRenderer with a Follow(ctx context.Context, records <-chan Record) method. In follow mode, stage_start records print immediately with the ▶ glyph: '▶ [stage-label] node-address'. When the matching stage_complete arrives, print the completion line with ✓ or ✗ glyph and duration: '✓ [stage-label] node-address (duration)'. The renderer must buffer the start timestamp keyed by (stage, node, task) to compute duration when the completion record arrives. audit_report_written records print indented below the most recent audit completion. The method returns when the context is cancelled or the records channel closes.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Summary Renderer is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify output format matches spec examples exactly: column alignment, glyph usage, audit report path indentation
